title: 2026 in film - Wikipedia
content: 2026 in film is an overview of events in the film industry scheduled to occur in 2026 Best Picture Academy Award-winner All Quiet on the Western Front entered the public domain this year Vitaphone celebrated its 100th anniversary Highest-grossing films The Super Mario Galaxy Movie † Project Hail Mary † Lionsgate / Universal The Devil Wears Prada 2 † Blades of the Guardians † The Super Mario film series surpassed 2 billion with the release of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie It became the first video game film franchise to surpass 2 billion Super Mario became the first animated film franchise with two films opening with more than 350 million worldwide.[3] Super Mario became the first animated film franchise with two films opening with more than 350 million worldwide.[3] Michael had the biggest opening weekend for a biopic with 217.4 million worldwide.[4] Michael had the biggest domestic opening weekend for a biopic with 97 million in the United States and Canada.[4] Michael had the biggest international opening for a biopic with 120.4 million from overseas markets.[4] Michael had the biggest domestic opening weekend for a biopic with 97 million in the United States and Canada.[4] Michael had the biggest international opening for a biopic with 120.4 million from overseas markets.[4] The Scream film series surpassed 1 billion with the release of Scream 7 It became the sixth horror film franchise to surpass 1 billion and the first in the slasher genre to surpass 1 billion Scream 7 became the highest-grossing film in the franchise, surpassing Scream (1996) Scream 7 became the highest-grossing film in the franchise, surpassing Scream (1996) The Pegasus film series surpassed 1 billion with the release of Pegasus 3 With 718.6 million views in the first 24 hours, Spider-Man: Brand New Day became the most-viewed trailer of all time, surpassing Deadpool & Wolverine (2024).[5] Scheduled award ceremonies 31st Critics' Choice Awards Critics Choice Association
